care.summary: Rau Răm (Vietnamese Coriander) is a fast-growing tropical herb prized for its distinctive spicy, lemony flavor. Thrives in warm, humid conditions with consistently moist soil and partial shade.
care.light: Partial shade to bright indirect light. Tolerates some morning sun but protect from intense afternoon heat. Can grow in full shade but may be less vigorous.
care.water: Keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged. Water when top inch of soil feels dry. Requires more frequent watering in warm weather. Can tolerate standing in shallow water like a semi-aquatic plant.
care.soil: Rich, well-draining potting mix with high organic matter. Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH (6.0-7.0). Mix in compost or aged manure for best growth.
care.humidity: High humidity (60-80%) preferred. Mist regularly or use humidity tray. Thrives in naturally humid environments like kitchens or bathrooms.
care.fertilizer: Feed every 2-3 weeks during growing season with balanced liquid fertilizer (10-10-10) or fish emulsion. Reduce to monthly in cooler months. Benefits from nitrogen-rich fertilizer for leafy growth.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic to humans and pets. Safe for culinary use. All parts of the plant are edible.
common_issues: Leaves turning brown or crispy - usually from low humidity or underwatering
Leggy growth - insufficient light, move to brighter location
Yellowing leaves - overwatering or poor drainage
Wilting despite moist soil - root rot from waterlogged conditions
Aphids or spider mites - treat with insecticidal soap or neem oil
Slow growth in winter - normal dormancy in cooler temperatures
tips: Harvest frequently by pinching stem tips to encourage bushier growth
Propagates easily from stem cuttings placed in water or moist soil
Flavor is strongest when leaves are used fresh; diminishes when cooked
Can be grown as a marginal water plant in ponds or water gardens
Protect from frost - bring indoors or treat as annual in cold climates
Companion plant with other moisture-loving herbs like mint or watercress
Regular harvesting prevents flowering and maintains tender leaf production
